FREE BIBLICAL STEWARDSHIP SCORECARD

How well does your financial life reflect your faith?

12 questions. 3 minutes. An honest diagnostic of where your money lines up with your convictions, and where the gaps are.

  • A personalized score across Theological Foundation and Practical Alignment
  • Your top three priorities, mapped to where your gaps actually are
  • Scripture and a plain-English explanation behind every question
  • A printable PDF you can revisit, share with your spouse, or bring to a planning conversation

Take the Scorecard

Free. No payment. No phone number. Your email gets you your results and occasional notes on biblical stewardship. Unsubscribe any time.

Does This Sound Like You?

Reactive Decisions

You feel like you’re often reacting with money. Choices get made out of fear, pressure, or necessity.

High Income, Low Purpose

You have a strong income, but it doesn’t feel like you’re getting ahead or using it intentionally.

Fragmented Team

You have professionals, but no one connects faith, family, taxes, and investing into one plan.

Scattered Financial World

Your giving, investing, and business decisions aren’t integrated, and opportunities slip by.

Craving Alignment

You want simplicity and biblical clarity so your finances reflect your faith and fuel Kingdom impact.

Most of the families I serve are W‑2 professionals earning $200k+, households with $500k+ in investable assets, or Christian business owners generating $1M+ in revenue.

See if We’re a Fit
“

I left the pulpit, but I never stopped pastoring families.

Helping devout Christian families turn wealth into lasting faithfulness.

Meet Andrew Zoll

I’m Andrew Zoll, a husband, homeschool dad of four, and fiduciary advisor to Christian families who want their money to match their mission. I left full‑time ministry to help believers steward wealth without guilt or confusion. Today I help families provide faithfully, invest confidently, and build a Kingdom legacy that outlives them.

I didn’t leave the pulpit because I stopped pastoring. I left because I saw a mission the Church kept outsourcing I couldn’t ignore.

During my years in ministry, I kept seeing the same pattern: conversations that started about stress or marriage or purpose often ended up circling back to money. Not greed. But guilt. Anxiety. Shame.

And when people looked to the Church for help, what did they get? A group class and some budget worksheets.

Most churches had outsourced biblical financial guidance to Financial Peace University. FPU is a good program, but it isn’t discipleship. It’s a rulebook.

And many pastors weren’t stepping in to teach what Scripture actually says because they were silently drowning themselves. Trapped by shame. Unable to teach what the Bible says because they felt disqualified by their own mess.

So God’s people were left vulnerable. Falling off the narrow road into one of two ditches:

  • Worshiping money (even if they wouldn’t call it that).
  • Fearing it like it was radioactive, convinced that poverty equals righteousness.

1 Timothy 6:10 gets misquoted.
Matthew 19:24 gets pulled out of context.
Verses like Proverbs 13:22 and Luke 16:9 get ignored entirely.

But the Bible doesn’t treat money as evil. It treats money as a mirror. A reflection of what we believe about God, ourselves, and eternity.

Once I saw that, I couldn’t unsee it.

So when my time in full‑time ministry came to a close, I asked the Lord what was next. I still loved preaching. Still loved teaching. But He made it clear: this was my next ministry.

Equipping faithful believers to stop feeling guilty about wealth and start stewarding it with clarity, courage, and conviction.

This is discipleship. It’s legacy. It’s Kingdom expansion.

And I don’t speak as someone who’s always gotten it right.

We financed a dog when we were broke newlyweds. I’ve carried student loans. I grew up in a household soaked in scarcity, where “just getting by” was the norm and risk was always too risky.

Rebecca and I are still breaking those chains. But Scripture has reshaped how we see money... not as a curse or a sign of compromise, but as a mirror and a tool for faithfulness.

Our theology didn’t adjust overnight. But like sanctification, it changed. Slowly. Deeply. And it stuck.

We're a classic Bible college love story: met freshman welcome week, married young, “ring by spring.” Today we homeschool our four loud, hilarious kids, and I serve families across the country who want their finances to match their faith.

This isn’t just what I do for a living.
It’s the ministry God has called me to.

Why Trust Me?

  • Former full‑time pastor (10+ years). I apply Scripture to real financial decisions, not just theory.
  • Author of How to Master Your Money God’s Way. Clear biblical principles, not sales scripts.
  • Registered investment advisor and insurance‑licensed. One guide who connects planning and implementation.
  • Experienced with $200k+ earners, $500k+ in investable assets, and $1M+ Christian business owners. Problems and strategies at your level.
  • Collaborates with your CPA and attorney so tax, giving, investing, business, and estate decisions work together.
See if We’re a Fit

Mission & Values

Stewardship as Discipleship

Money decisions are spiritual decisions. We align plans with Scripture first, knowing that stewardship is how we worship with what we’ve been given.

Clarity over Complexity

You don’t need a stack of binders or 42-page PDFs. You need a clear plan you understand and trust. Simplicity is a stewardship virtue.

Kingdom ROI

We plan with eternity in mind, aiming to maximize the return on what matters most.

Bold Conviction

We don’t tiptoe around truth. We coach with courage and care, helping believers build and deploy resources to the glory of God with courage and conviction.

See if We’re a Fit

How We Help

Ongoing Financial Discipleship

Ongoing biblical financial planning for Christian families who want wise counsel, accountability, and a plan anchored in Scripture. We walk with you across giving, investing, taxes, risk, and legacy so your money serves your mission.

Purpose-Driven Investment Management

Biblically aligned portfolios with integrated planning and clear communication. Built for households who want stewardship and excellence side by side.

For families with $500k+ in investable assets.

One-Time Stewardship Blueprint

A concentrated planning engagement to clarify where you stand, what Scripture calls you to prioritize, and the next steps to take.

Deliverable: a written, actionable plan you can implement.

What this covers

  • Custom tax strategy
  • Biblical risk & protection
  • Charitable & legacy design
  • Purpose-driven investing
  • Stewardship coaching
  • Retirement income planning
  • Family provision guidance
  • Business stewardship
  • Money mindset coaching
See if We’re a Fit

Our 3‑Step Process

Here’s how we turn biblical wisdom into practical guidance.

1

Stewardship Clarity Call (25 min)

A focused conversation to get to know one another, confirm fit, answer your top questions, and outline next steps.

2

Stewardship Blueprint

A deep‑dive analysis and written plan that connects your living, giving, investing, taxes, and legacy. Rooted in Scripture, not sales.

3

Ongoing Guidance

Quarterly reviews, real‑time access, and an annual Kingdom Audit to keep your plan aligned with Scripture.

See if We’re a Fit

Still Have Questions?

Quick answers to what tends to come up before a first call.

It starts with the conviction that money is a tool for faithfulness, not the goal. Every recommendation flows from Scripture, not market trends.

In practice, that means we plan giving, saving, investing, taxes, and legacy under one purpose: honoring God with what He’s entrusted to you. The result is a plan that strengthens your faith as much as your finances.

A focused, pastoral conversation about your financial situation, your goals, and what biblical stewardship could look like for your family. A general knowledge of your financial position is enough. You don’t need paperwork or perfect answers.

I’ll guide you with questions like:

  • What does faithful stewardship look like to you right now?
  • How confident do you feel about your current plan?
  • What role does Scripture play in your financial decisions?
  • If everything were aligned, what would peace with money look like?

By the end, you’ll have a clearer picture of where you stand and what a next faithful step could be, whether that includes working with me or not.

Because the first step should be simple. This is a short conversation to get to know one another, understand your situation, and give you a clear next step. Not a full planning session.

If your situation needs more depth, we’ll schedule a longer follow-up. Think of this as a conversation, not a commitment.

Most advisors start with numbers. I start with Scripture.

No jargon, no sales pressure, no binders you’ll never read. My process connects your entire financial life under one biblical approach: living, giving, investing, taxes, and legacy. The goal isn’t beating the market. It’s building clarity, confidence, and conviction in how you steward what God has given you.

I left full-time ministry to serve families exactly this way.

No. You always stay in control. My role is counsel, not control.

I serve as a fiduciary, accountable to you and before God for the guidance I give. Some families delegate investment management or tax strategy so they can focus on family and ministry. Others stay hands-on while I provide direction. Either way, you decide.

Yes. Stumbling in some area of stewardship is part of the Christian life. This call is about faithfulness, not perfection.

I’ve made financial mistakes too (including financing a dog when my wife and I were broke newlyweds). What matters is where you’re headed and whether your next step honors the Lord.

More common than you’d think. Money reveals differences in values, fears, and communication, not just math.

I help both spouses see where your beliefs, priorities, and goals line up (and where they don’t). The focus is shared conviction: a financial plan that reflects your joint mission before the Lord. The healthiest plans are built when husband and wife are moving in the same direction.

Let’s Steward This Well

If you’ve made it this far, chances are this call is for you.

It’s short. It’s free. And it could reshape how you think about both money and faith forever.

Pick a time that works. I’ll meet you there.


Resources

Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be still wiser; teach a righteous man, and he will increase in learning.
-Proverbs 9:9
